file.content: # WP-052 — EDT-V3-04: Single-letter shortcuts surfaced in toolbar tooltips **Branch:** `feat/v3-edt-v3-04-shortcut-chips` **Effort:** S — 3 days **Status:** `now` (no deps) --- ## Required reading Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code. - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §5 T2 EDT-V3-04 entry; §3 Design Pillars D3, D8; anti-pattern A5; §A Agent Instructions - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md` - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/tools/PlanToolbar.tsx` — or wherever plan-view toolbar tools are registered; this is the primary target - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/tools/modifierBar.ts` — modifier bar (recently added); reference for tool registration pattern --- ## Verbatim EDT-V3-04 tracker entry #### EDT-V3-04 — Single-letter shortcuts surfaced in toolbar tooltips **Status.** `now`. R-A "What we'd copy" #2. **Scope.** Every toolbar button shows its single-letter shortcut as a chip in the tooltip AND as an 8 px chip in the bottom-right corner of the icon (always visible, no hover). Shortcuts are already wired in code — this WP surfaces them in chrome. **Data model.** Each tool registration gains a `shortcut: string` field used for both keyboard binding and chip label. **UI.** 8 × 8 px chip in `--text-2xs`, `--color-muted-foreground` on `--surface-2`, bottom-right corner of icon. Tooltip: "Wall (W)" with W chip. **Acceptance.** Wall tool tooltip reads "Wall (W)" with `W` chip permanently visible on icon. Door/Window/Dimension/Phase show `D`/`WI`/`DI`/`PH`. Visual regression confirms placement. **Effort.** S — 3 days. --- ## Setup ```bash cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai git fetch origin git checkout main git pull --ff-only git checkout -b feat/v3-edt-v3-04-shortcut-chips uv sync && pnpm install ``` --- ## Implementation tasks ### 1. Audit existing tool registrations Find all toolbar tool definitions — they may be in `PlanToolbar.tsx`, a `tools.ts` config file, or similar. List the tools and their current keyboard shortcuts. At minimum these tools must have shortcut chips: | Tool | Shortcut | |------|---------| | Wall | `W` | | Door | `D` | | Window | `WI` | | Dimension | `DI` | | Phase | `PH` | | Stair | `S` | | Roof | `R` | | Column | `C` | | Detail Region | `DR` | Add a `shortcut?: string` field to the tool definition type if not already present. ### 2. `ShortcutChip` component (`packages/web/src/ui/ShortcutChip.tsx`) ```tsx export function ShortcutChip({ label }: { label: string }) { return ( {label} ); } ``` No hex literals — tokens only. ### 3. Wire `ShortcutChip` into toolbar buttons In the toolbar button component (wherever `PlanToolbar.tsx` renders individual tool buttons): - Wrap the icon container in `position: relative` - Render `` inside if `tool.shortcut` is defined - Update the tooltip text from `"Wall"` to `"Wall (W)"` when shortcut is present Pattern: ```tsx ``` ### 4. Keyboard binding audit Verify the keyboard shortcuts listed above are actually bound. If a shortcut is in the tool definition but not wired to a `keydown` handler, add the binding in the existing keyboard handler (usually in a `useToolKeyBindings` hook or similar). Two-letter shortcuts (`WI`, `DI`, `PH`, `DR`) use a chord: within 400 ms of pressing the first key, pressing the second activates the tool. If no chord infrastructure exists, add a simple `pendingChord: string | null` state in the keyboard handler. ### 5. Modifier bar shortcut display If `packages/web/src/tools/modifierBar.ts` exposes modifier keys (e.g. `L` for loop mode), ensure those also follow the chip pattern when rendered in the OptionsBar. ### 6. Tests No Python tests. Add a lightweight snapshot or unit test confirming: - `ShortcutChip` renders with the correct label - Tool definitions for Wall/Door/Window include a `shortcut` field - Tooltip string includes `(W)` suffix when shortcut is present --- ## Implementation rules - **No scope creep.** Do not wire loop-mode modifier here (EDT-V3-05). Do not add new keyboard shortcuts beyond auditing existing ones. - **Chips are always visible** — no hover required. D3: every glyph carries meaning. - **No hex literals** in any `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`. - A5 antidote: keyboard is discoverable by reading the toolbar, not by hunting tooltips. --- ## Verify gate ```bash cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai make verify pnpm exec tsc --noEmit ``` --- ## Commit and push ```bash git add packages/web/src/ui/ShortcutChip.tsx \ packages/web/src/tools/PlanToolbar.tsx \ packages/web/src/tools/modifierBar.ts git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF' feat(edt): EDT-V3-04 single-letter shortcut chips on toolbar icons ShortcutChip component (always-visible 8px chip bottom-right of icon); wired into PlanToolbar tool buttons; tooltip updated to "Tool (X)" format; chord support for two-letter shortcuts (WI, DI, PH, DR). Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 EOF )" git push -u origin feat/v3-edt-v3-04-shortcut-chips ``` Do NOT open a PR and do NOT merge to main. --- ## Final report template ``` WP: EDT-V3-04 Prompt file: spec/v3-prompts/wp-052.md Branch: feat/v3-edt-v3-04-shortcut-chips Final commit: Verify: green | red — Acceptance: yes | partial — | no Files touched: Lines: + - As-shipped notes: <2-3 sentences> Follow-up surfaced: ```

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	/**
2	 * Tool registry — spec §16.
3	 *
4	 * Single source of truth for the §16 floating tool palette.
5	 *
6	 *   - `TOOL_REGISTRY` lists every primary drawing tool with its spec'd
7	 *     hotkey, lucide icon name (resolved via `Icons[icon]`), and per-mode
8	 *     visibility.
9	 *   - `paletteForMode(mode)` returns the ordered tool buttons for a
10	 *     given workspace mode (Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, etc.).
11	 *   - `isToolDisabled(tool, document)` lets the palette dim tools that
12	 *     can't activate (e.g. Floor before any walls exist).
13	 *
14	 * Per-tool grammar specs (location-line cycle, chain mode, sketch-vs-
15	 * pick, etc.) live in `tools/<tool>.ts` and are referenced from this
16	 * registry by the `kind` discriminator.
17	 */
18

359	export function paletteForMode(mode: WorkspaceMode, t: TFunction): ToolDefinition[] {
360	  const registry = getToolRegistry(t);
361	  return PALETTE_ORDER.map((id) => registry[id]).filter((tool) => tool.modes.includes(mode));
362	}
363	
364	export interface ToolDisabledContext {
365	  hasAnyWall: boolean;
366	  hasAnyFloor: boolean;
367	  hasAnySelection: boolean;
368	}
369	
370	export function isToolDisabled(
371	  toolId: ToolId,
372	  ctx: ToolDisabledContext,
373	  t: TFunction,
374	): { disabled: boolean; reason?: string } {
375	  switch (toolId) {
376	    case 'floor':
377	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall) return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.drawWallFirst') };
378	      return { disabled: false };
379	    case 'floor-sketch':
380	      // Sketch mode authors a floor from a boundary loop; it does not depend on walls.
381	      return { disabled: false };
382	    case 'roof-sketch':

3	 *
4	 * `ToolModifierDescriptor` is the contract each authoring tool registers to
5	 * declare which modifier chips appear in the 36 px secondary contextual bar.
6	 * The bar's layout is identical across all tools; only the descriptors differ.
7	 *
8	 * Modifier vocabulary (spec §UX table):
9	 *   toggle       — on/off flag; may be sticky per session via toolPrefs
10	 *   cycle        — cycles through a named value set (e.g. wall location line,
11	 *                  door swing side); current value stored in toolPrefs when
12	 *                  sticky === true
13	 *   always-armed — permanently active; shown as an informational chip only
14	 *                  (Numeric input, Tab-cycle)
15	 */

270	export function nextCycleValue(descriptor: CycleModifierDescriptor, current: string): string {
271	  const idx = descriptor.values.indexOf(current);
